---
title: CLI & SDK
description: The open-managed-agents Node CLI and the @openma/sdk TypeScript client — your two ways to talk to the API without curl.
---

import { Tabs, TabItem, Aside, LinkCard } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

openma ships two developer-facing packages:

- **`@openma/cli`** — a Node CLI for scripting against the API.
- **`@openma/sdk`** — a typed TypeScript client. Wraps REST + SSE; one async iterator per turn; runs anywhere `fetch` exists (Node ≥ 20, Bun, Deno, browsers, Cloudflare Workers).

Both live in this repo (`packages/cli`, `packages/sdk`).

## CLI

### Install

<Tabs syncKey="pkg-mgr">
  <TabItem label="npm">
    ```bash
    npm i -g @openma/cli
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="pnpm">
    ```bash
    pnpm add -g @openma/cli
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="bun">
    ```bash
    bun add -g @openma/cli
    ```
  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

### Configure

The CLI reads two env vars:

```bash
export OMA_BASE_URL="https://app.openma.dev"
export OMA_API_KEY="oma_..."
```

For self-hosters, point `OMA_BASE_URL` at your own deployment.

### Common commands

```bash
# List agents
oma agents list

# Create an agent from a JSON file
oma agents create -f ./my-agent.json

# Start a session
oma sessions create --agent agent_abc123 --env env_abc123 --title "research"

# Chat — post a turn AND stream the reply token-by-token to your terminal
oma sessions chat sess_xyz "Summarize the openma docs."

# Fire-and-forget message (no streaming back; useful when something else is tailing)
oma sessions message sess_xyz "Now also include the integrations."

# Tail a session's full event stream (SSE under the hood)
oma sessions tail sess_xyz

# Replay a session's full event log
oma sessions logs sess_xyz

# Show your usage
oma usage
```

Run `oma --help` or `oma <command> --help` for the full surface.

<Aside type="tip" title="Pipe-friendly">
  CLI subcommands accept `--json` and emit machine-readable output. Pipe into `jq` for ad-hoc analysis.
</Aside>

## SDK

`@openma/sdk` is a typed TypeScript client. Discriminated-union events, async-iterator streaming, runs on Node ≥ 20, Bun, Deno, browsers, and Cloudflare Workers (anywhere `fetch` exists).

### Install

<Tabs syncKey="pkg-mgr">
  <TabItem label="npm">
    ```bash
    npm i @openma/sdk
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="pnpm">
    ```bash
    pnpm add @openma/sdk
    ```
  </TabItem>
  <TabItem label="bun">
    ```bash
    bun add @openma/sdk
    ```
  </TabItem>
</Tabs>

### Quick start

```ts
import { OpenMA } from "@openma/sdk";

const oma = new OpenMA({ apiKey: process.env.OMA_API_KEY! });

// Streaming chat — async iterator over typed events.
for await (const ev of oma.sessions.chat(sessionId, "Hello")) {
  if (ev.type === "agent.message_chunk") process.stdout.write(ev.delta);
}
```

### Three streams, one channel

Text, thinking, and tool-input deltas all flow over the same SSE iterator. Each carries a correlation id (`message_id`, `thinking_id`, `tool_use_id`) that matches the eventually-committed canonical event:

```ts
for await (const ev of oma.sessions.chat(sessionId, "Use bash to print uptime")) {
  switch (ev.type) {
    case "agent.message_chunk":      // live text delta
      process.stdout.write(ev.delta); break;
    case "agent.thinking_chunk":     // live extended-thinking delta
      process.stderr.write(`💭 ${ev.delta}`); break;
    case "agent.tool_use":           // canonical tool call (id matches any prior input chunks)
      console.log(`→ ${ev.name}`, ev.input); break;
    case "agent.tool_result":
      console.log(`← ${ev.content}`); break;
    case "session.warning":          // recovery scan flagged an interrupted stream
      console.warn(`⚠ ${ev.source}: ${ev.message}`); break;
    case "session.status_idle":
      return;                        // turn done; server closes the stream
  }
}
```

### chatComplete — accumulated turn

When you don't need token-by-token rendering:

```ts
const reply = await oma.sessions.chatComplete(sessionId, "Hello", {
  onText: (delta) => process.stdout.write(delta), // optional incremental hook
});

console.log(reply.text);          // assembled assistant text
console.log(reply.thinking);      // string[] — one per reasoning block
console.log(reply.toolCalls);     // {id, name, input}[]
console.log(reply.toolResults);   // {tool_use_id, content, is_error?}[]
```

### tail — long-lived monitor

```ts
// Replays history on connect, then streams every future event. Never
// closes; pass an AbortSignal or break out to stop.
for await (const ev of oma.sessions.tail(sessionId, { signal: ac.signal })) {
  console.log(ev.type, ev);
}
```

### Errors

```ts
import { OpenMAError } from "@openma/sdk";

try {
  await oma.sessions.get("sess-bogus");
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof OpenMAError) {
    console.error(err.status, err.body); // typed body when the server returns JSON
    if (err.status === 404) { /* ... */ }
  }
}
```

### Resources covered

| Resource | Methods |
|---|---|
| `oma.agents` | `list`, `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete` |
| `oma.sessions` | `list`, `get`, `create`, `chat`, `chatComplete`, `tail`, `events`, `message`, `interrupt`, `archive`, `delete` |
| `oma.environments` | `list`, `get`, `create`, `delete` |

More resources land per release — file an issue if something you need is missing.

<Aside type="tip" title="Or just call fetch">
  Every endpoint is plain JSON or SSE. If you want zero dependencies, point `fetch` at the [REST API](/build/api/) directly — the SDK is sugar, not a requirement. The `@openma/cli` source (`packages/cli/src/`) is a small reference implementation you can lift.
</Aside>
